tasks.title: Import a Project and Add Users

steps.action: Click the Projects tab.
Click New Project.
Select Attach a File and open W:\Course Files\CBFD-OVER\PTC_Electric_Car.zip.
Click Next.

Observe the values in the Name and Key fields.
Note: The Key is a string that can be appended to tracker items to easily identify their project identity.
Ensure the checkboxes are selected for Include all Work Items and Documents and Include Wiki pages.
Click Finish.

Click the vertical ellipsis (?) in the Admin tab and select Members.
Note: Vertical ellipses often display a dropdown list in Codebeamer.
From the toolbar, click Add new Member.
In the Add new Member dialog under Step 1, type yut and select Yuta Systems Engineer when the suggestion pop-up dialog appears.
Next to yuta, type k and then select Kartini Requirements Engineer when the suggestion pop-up
dialog appears.
Under Step 2, select the checkboxes for Developer and Tester.
Click Add.
Click Add new Member.
Under Step 1, type jiw and select Jiwan Release Manager.
Under Step 2, select the checkbox for Stakeholder.
Click Add.
Click the Trackers tab.

Observe the tracker tree to the left and a dashboard with widgets to the right.
Note: The concepts of trackers and dashboards in Codebeamer will be discussed in more detail later.
In the tracker tree, double-click Customer Requirement Specifications to open the customer requirements tracker.

Observe the three requirements in the tracker.
Note: These are requirements for the electric car design as supplied to the project team by the customer.
From the toolbar, click the Settings (gear) icon.
In the Settings dialog, select the checkboxes for Work Item Key-IDs and Numbering.
Click Save.

Observe the changes to requirements in the tracker tree.
Note: The tracker items (customer requirements) in the list are now numbered. They also bear a unique Key ID that identifies them as belonging to the Customer Requirement Specifications tracker and have a unique index number for that tracker.
Note: These settings will now apply to all trackers display views.
